mission.title: falco: Expose the node metadata in the Falco outputs API
mission.description: Expose the node metadata in the Falco outputs API. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
**What would you like to be added**:

Falco should expose the "node" metadata (host that originated an alert) to third-party consumers.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Expose the node metadata in the Falco outputs API
steps.description:
This PR adds a new field (hostname) in gRPC event messages.
In theory, it adds a new field without causing trouble to old clients. It also passes hostname to other outputs but it does nothing. This was done to keep consistency between methods signatures. Maybe we want to move the field to `outputFields`

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Expose the node metadata in the Falco outputs API" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR adds a new field (hostname) in gRPC event messages.
In theory, it adds a new field without causing trouble to old clients. It also passes hostname to other outputs but it does nothing. This was done to keep consistency between methods signatures. Maybe we want to move the field to `outputFields`

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
